svg.text: A disagreement cools down the moment one person reconstructs the other side fairly before responding.
Start with the concrete case: A disagreement cools down the moment one person reconstructs the other side fairly before responding.
Name the hidden structure: Weak caricature vs fair reconstruction comparison panel.
Watch for the trap: Critiquing the weakest version of an opposing view.
Transfer the habit immediately: Take an argument you dislike and rewrite it in the strongest fair form you can manage.

Unlike broad intro courses, this lesson turns the principle of charity into a practical operating skill through a real scenario, a visual model, a retrieval gate, and a transfer task built around argument mapping, evidence discipline, and fair-minded judgment.
Critiquing the weakest version of an opposing view.

Take an argument you dislike and rewrite it in the strongest fair form you can manage.
The goal is not to memorize the label, but to practice a repeatable move in real judgment. Ask the learner to produce a concrete artifact, not just an opinion.
